_doc: Allowlisted perturbation knobs for the Autoresearch agent. The agent (NIM 8B) must propose exactly one perturbation per iteration as a JSON object {knob, new_value, reason}, where knob is one of the keys below and new_value satisfies the matching constraint. Anything outside this menu is blocked at the rails before any train.py mutation happens. This menu is the *safe interface*: the agent cannot write Python, can only twist a pre-defined knob.
_arms_race_logic: If the agent finds a way to set a knob to a value that crashes training, the next-iteration prompt will see the crash + reason and the agent will avoid it. Crash != safety violation. Rails are about preventing the agent from doing things that would hurt the host (file writes outside scratch, shell escape, infinite memory) — they are NOT about preventing bad training runs.

d_model.doc: hidden dim (must be divisible by n_head)

cross_constraints.doc: TransformerEngine attention requires d_model to be divisible by n_head — guaranteed by menu choices, included as defense in depth
The Autoresearch evaluator runs 30 training steps; warmup must leave at least 5 post-warmup steps for the cosine decay schedule to mean anything
